index.title: Fullstack Web Developer
index.tip: Use the left and right arrows to navigate smoothly through the site.
index.tipmobile: Swipe right to move forward on the site, and left to go back.

section1.intro: Hello, my name is Chris Ngabala, and I am a Fullstack developer passionate about web development.

section2.title: My Journey
section2.content:
My journey began at the Multimedia School, where I gained solid training in programming and web development. 

 In my first year, I learned the fundamental basics of coding, which allowed me to build a solid foundation in HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. 

  This foundation then prepared me to tackle more complex and advanced topics during my second year.

section3.title: My Expertise
section3.content:
In my second year, I significantly expanded my skills by exploring a variety of technologies and frameworks, allowing me to develop more complex projects:

 - Back-End: PHP, MySQL, Symfony, and SQLite for backend development, including serverless solutions like Firebase.

 - Front-End: React/Native, TypeScript, Node.js for frontend development.

section4.title: Goals and Aspirations
section4.content:
Currently, I am looking for an internship starting in September 2024 (1 week at school / 3 weeks in the company) in view of my third academic year at Yutopia Meta Campus Paris. 

  I am particularly interested in opportunities that will allow me to work on innovative and challenging projects while continuing to develop my skills as a Fullstack developer.

project.title: My Projects
project.section1.content1: Languages used: Jimdo(CMS), HTML, CSS, JavaScript
project.section1.content2: I worked as a Front-End web developer with the goal of redesigning the site. I handled the new design of the site via the Jimdo CMS and customized it with interactive JavaScript widgets.
project.section2.content1: Languages used: HTML, CSS, JavaScript, Firebase, React, TypeScript
project.section2.content2: I worked as a Front-End web developer with the goal of redesigning the site. I handled the new design of the site via the Jimdo CMS and customized it with interactive JavaScript widgets.
project.section3.content1: Languages used: CSS, JavaScript, React, Symfony, SQLite
project.section3.content2: For this individual school project, the goal was to develop a site allowing the user to define their name and create, modify, delete, and categorize vinyl records. The project is divided into two parts: a user interface in React and a back-end in Symfony, connected via RESTful APIs, with SQLite as the database.
